source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure Essential Contacts is Configured for Organization

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure Essential Contacts is Configured for Organization
input_row.description: It is recommended that Essential Contacts is configured to designate email addresses for Google Cloud services to notify of important technical or security information.
input_row.rationale: Many Google Cloud services, such as Cloud Billing, send out notifications to share important information with Google Cloud users. By default, these notifications are sent to members with certain Identity and Access Management (IAM) roles. With Essential Contacts, you can customize who receives notifications by providing your own list of contacts. Impact: There is no charge for Essential Contacts except for the 'Technical Incidents' category that is only available to premium support customers.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Essential Contacts by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/iam- admin/essential-contacts 2. Make sure the organization appears in the resource selector at the top of the page. The resource selector tells you what project, folder, or organization you are currently managing contacts for. 3. Ensure that appropriate email addresses are configured for each of the following notification categories: • Legal • Security • Suspension • Technical Alternatively, appropriate email addresses can be configured for the All notification category to receive all possible important notifications. From Google Cloud CLI 1. To list all configured organization Essential Contacts run a command: gcloud essential-contacts list --organization=<ORGANIZATION_ID> 2. Ensure at least one appropriate email address is configured for each of the following notification categories: • LEGAL • SECURITY • SUSPENSION • TECHNICAL Alternatively, appropriate email addresses can be configured for the ALL notification category to receive all possible important notifications.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Essential Contacts by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/iam- admin/essential-contacts 2. Make sure the organization appears in the resource selector at the top of the page. The resource selector tells you what project, folder, or organization you are currently managing contacts for. 3. Click +Add contact 4. In the Email and Confirm Email fields, enter the email address of the contact. 5. From the Notification categories drop-down menu, select the notification categories that you want the contact to receive communications for. 6. Click Save From Google Cloud CLI 1. To add an organization Essential Contacts run a command: gcloud essential-contacts create --email="<EMAIL>" \ --notification-categories="<NOTIFICATION_CATEGORIES>" \ --organization=<ORGANIZATION_ID> Default Value: By default, there are no Essential Contacts configured. In the absence of an Essential Contact, the following IAM roles are used to identify users to notify for the following categories: • Legal : roles/billing.admin • Security : roles/resourcemanager.organizationAdmin • Suspension : roles/owner • Technical : roles/owner • Technical Incidents : roles/owner References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/managing-notification-contacts
input_row.impact: There is no charge for Essential Contacts except for the 'Technical Incidents' category that is only available to premium support customers.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

- The audit field provides a clear command to query the essential contacts using the Google Cloud CLI, indicating that this control can be verified programmatically.
- The control involves querying a property (essential contacts) of the GCP organization through available GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud), which are part of the standard services and configurations offered by GCP.
- The Google Cloud CLI is specifically mentioned in the audit instructions, providing a direct command (`gcloud essential-contacts list --organization=<ORGANIZATION_ID>`) to verify if the Essential Contacts are configured.
- The task is related to a resource management aspect of GCP, making it suitable for automated verification.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_organization_essential_contacts_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- Use Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) with the CLI command `gcloud essential-contacts list --organization=<ORGANIZATION_ID>` to obtain the list of essential contacts configured for a specific organization.
- Enumerate the Essential Contacts configuration for the specified organization.
- Validate that at least one email address exists for each of the required notification categories: LEGAL, SECURITY, SUSPENSION, and TECHNICAL. Alternatively, ensure there is a contact for the ALL category, which covers all possible notifications.
- Configuration properties/settings to validate include the presence of emails appropriately assigned to the notification categories.
- Pass/Fail Criteria:
  - **Pass:** At least one email is configured for each necessary category or for the ALL category.
  - **Fail:** Missing emails for any of the notification categories and none configured for the ALL category.
  
This approach ensures that the organization has designated contacts who will be notified for key events and actions by Google Cloud services, supporting compliance and operational awareness.
step2_review:
---

### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. The presence and configuration of Essential Contacts at the organization level can be programmatically verified using GCP APIs and the `gcloud` CLI.
- **GCP SDK Capabilities:**  
  The `gcloud essential-contacts` command group is available and supports listing contacts at the organization level. The underlying API (`essentialcontacts.googleapis.com`) is accessible and supports these queries.
- **CloudFormation/GCP Config:**  
  GCP Config (now called Config Controller) does not natively support Essential Contacts as a resource type, so the CLI/API is the correct approach.
- **Conclusion:**  
  This control can and should be checked via GCP APIs/SDK/CLI, not manually.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 9/10**

- **Automation Details:**  
  - The command `gcloud essential-contacts list --organization=<ORGANIZATION_ID>` is correct and returns the configured contacts.
  - The logic to check for at least one email per required notification category (LEGAL, SECURITY, SUSPENSION, TECHNICAL) or for the ALL category is accurate and aligns with GCP documentation.
- **gcloud Methods/CLI Validity:**  
  - The command is valid and supported.
  - The output includes `notification_categories` and `email` fields, allowing for programmatic validation.
- **Manual Steps:**  
  - Not applicable; all steps are automatable.
- **Program Name Convention:**  
  - The program name `gcp_organization_essential_contacts_configured` is descriptive but could be more specific (see Improvements below).
- **Minor Issue:**  
  - The assessment does not explicitly mention service account permissions required (e.g., `Essential Contacts Viewer` role on the organization), which is relevant for automation.

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Missing Considerations:**
  - **Permissions:**  
    The automation will require the service account or user running the script to have `roles/essentialcontacts.viewer` at the organization level. This should be explicitly mentioned.
  - **API Alternative:**  
    The REST API (`essentialcontacts.googleapis.com/v1/organizations/{org_id}/contacts`) can be used for direct integration or custom tooling.
  - **Edge Case:**  
    If contacts are only configured at the project or folder level, this will not be detected by the org-level command. The control is about org-level, but a note about scope could be useful.
  - **Notification Categories:**  
    The list of categories should be validated against the latest GCP documentation, as categories may evolve.
- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  - To be more explicit, consider:  
    `gcp_organization_essential_contacts_notification_categories_configured`
    or  
    `gcp_organization_essential_contacts_per_category_configured`
  - This clarifies that the check is per notification category, not just for the presence of any contact.
- **Output Validation:**  
  - Recommend parsing the output and explicitly reporting which categories are missing, not just pass/fail.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The approach is technically sound, aligns with GCP best practices, and leverages supported APIs/CLI commands. Minor improvements are recommended for completeness and clarity.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **Modify**

- **Keep as Automated.**
- **Modify** the automation details to:
  - Explicitly mention required IAM permissions.
  - Suggest parsing and reporting missing categories.
  - Optionally, update the program name for clarity as suggested above.
  - Reference the REST API as an alternative for environments where CLI is not available or for integration into custom compliance tooling.

---

**Summary Table:**

| Section             | Verdict / Score | Notes                                                                                  |
|---------------------|-----------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation | AGREE           | Automated is correct; gcloud/API supports this.                                        |
| Technical Accuracy  | 9/10            | Command and logic are correct; minor improvements possible.                            |
| Improvements        | -               | Add IAM note, clarify output, consider REST API, improve program name.                 |
| Confidence          | HIGH            | Approach is robust and industry-standard.                                              |
| Final Recommendation| Modify          | Keep automated, but clarify details and improve naming/output as above.                |

---

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identified that the configuration of Essential Contacts at the organization level can be programmatically audited using the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud essential-contacts list --organization=<ORGANIZATION_ID>`) or the underlying API. The required properties (emails and notification categories) are directly exposed via the API/CLI, allowing for a fully automated compliance check. Manual inspection is unnecessary as all relevant data is accessible and verifiable through GCP's supported tools.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
gcp_essential_contacts_organization_notification_categories_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- GCP Resource/Service: Essential Contacts at the Organization level
- API/CLI Method: Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command:  
  `gcloud essential-contacts list --organization=<ORGANIZATION_ID>`
- Properties to Check:
  - Each contact's `email` field
  - Each contact's `notification_categories` field (should include: LEGAL, SECURITY, SUSPENSION, TECHNICAL; or ALL)
- Pass Criteria:
  - At least one email address is configured for each required notification category (LEGAL, SECURITY, SUSPENSION, TECHNICAL), or a contact exists with the ALL category.
- Fail Criteria:
  - Any required notification category (LEGAL, SECURITY, SUSPENSION, TECHNICAL) is missing a configured contact email, and there is no contact with the ALL category.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
There were no disagreements; both AI opinions supported automation and provided technically accurate reasoning.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
